---
title: "Datos Espaciales"
author: "Lucas Melfi"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}
install.packages("sf")
install.packages("rnaturalearth")
install.packages("pak")
install.packages("tidyverse")
library(rnaturalearth)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)


```


```{r}

pais <- ne_states("Argentina")

mapa <- pais |> 
  # seleccionar columnas
  select(pais = admin,
         region = name_es,
         geometry)
mapa


```
```{r}
mapa |> 
  ggplot() +
  geom_sf(fill = "#9069C0", 
          color = "#EBD2FA",
          linewidth = 0.4)
```
```{r}
mapa_centroide <- mapa |> 
  select(region, geometry) |> #selecciona las columnas region y geometry
  mutate(centroide = st_centroid(geometry)) #crea la columna centroide y pone ahi el calculo del centroide 
```

```{r}
mapa_centroide_coordenadas <- mapa_centroide |> 
  mutate(lon = st_coordinates(centroide)[,1], #crea la columna lon y ahi pone la coordenada 1(longitud)
         lat = st_coordinates(centroide)[,2]) #crea la columna lat y ahi pone la coordenada 2 (latitud)
```

#GRafico del centroide

```{r}
mapa_centroide |> 
  filter(region == mapa$region[3]) |> 
  ggplot() +
  # mapa
  geom_sf(fill = "#9069C0", 
          linewidth = NA) +
  # capa del punto
  geom_sf(
    aes(geometry = centroide),
    color = "#402E5A",
    size = 3, alpha = 1) +
  # texto bajo el punto
  geom_sf_text(
    aes(geometry = centroide),
    label = "centroide Jujuy", size = 3,
    vjust = 2)
```
#Buffer

```{r}
mapa_buffer <- mapa |> 
  # filtrar una región/polígono
  filter(region == mapa$region[3]) |> 
  # crear buffer
  mutate(buffer = st_buffer(geometry, 
                            dist = 20000, #distancia del buffer
                            max_cells = 10000)) #poligonos
```

```{r}
mapa_buffer |> 
  ggplot() +
  # capa de la región
  geom_sf(aes(geometry = geometry), 
          fill = "#9069C0", linewidth = NA) +
  # capa del buffer
  geom_sf(aes(geometry = buffer), 
          fill = "#9069C0", alpha = 0.5, linewidth = NA)
```
